src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.general_idea: Refactor HttpSecurity configuration to use the new Spring Security 6.x OAuth2 Authorization Server DSL, replacing the deprecated OAuth2AuthorizationServerConfigurer and manual endpoint matching.
Remove the custom OidcUserInfo mapper bean and configuration, likely relying on default behavior or a different implementation strategy in the new version.
src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.human_impl: Removed Function<OidcUserInfoAuthenticationContext, OidcUserInfo> userInfoMapper and associated .oidc().userInfoEndpoint() configuration.

src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.general_idea: Simplify the authorization request rules by removing specific permitAll paths and role-based checks, defaulting to authenticated for all requests within the security matcher scope.
Replace implicit form login configuration with explicit exception handling to redirect unauthenticated HTML requests to the login page.
Introduce a new bean for RegisteredClientRepository using a custom implementation that likely integrates with the application's persistence layer and caching.
Introduce a new bean for ClientRegistrationRepository using a custom Jdbc implementation.
Change the SecurityFilterChain order from Ordered.HIGHEST_PRECEDENCE to explicit integer 1, which is functionally equivalent but uses the newer preferred style.
Add @EnableWebSecurity annotation to the configuration class, ensuring web security features are explicitly enabled in the new Spring Security version.
Replace manual SimpleCacheManager with Spring's dedicated CaffeineCacheManager to leverage built-in registration and configuration features.
src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.human_impl: Changed from `new SimpleCacheManager()` with manual `ArrayList<CaffeineCache>` population to `new CaffeineCacheManager()` using `registerCustomCache`.

src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.general_idea: Explicitly disable null values in the cache manager to prevent potential runtime issues or unexpected behavior with null entries.
Add logic to handle cache entries with a TTL of zero by omitting the expiration policy, effectively creating non-expiring caches for those entries.
Migrate Jackson imports from com.fasterxml.jackson to tools.jackson to align with Spring Boot 3.5+ dependency changes.
Update JsonGenerator creation to use the new API method createGenerator on ObjectMapper instead of getFactory().createGenerator.
Migrate Jackson imports from com.fasterxml.jackson to tools.jackson to align with Spring Boot 3.5+ dependencies.
Update JsonGenerator creation to use the new API method available in tools.jackson.
Replace string-based charset encoding with StandardCharsets.UTF_8 to remove checked exception handling.
Update log statement to use parameterized logging instead of string concatenation.
Capture and store client_id in login history for better audit trails.
Replace explicit cast after instanceof check with Java 16+ pattern matching for cleaner code.
Replace Apache Commons StringUtils.equalsIgnoreCase with Java 9+ Strings.CI.equals for case-insensitive comparison.
Remove manual null check for loadUserByUsername result, relying on Spring Security's internal contract enforcement.
Remove debug log statements for authentication failures to reduce noise or align with new logging standards.
src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.human_impl: Removed this.logger.debug("Authentication failed: no credentials provided");

Extract client_id from the saved request parameters to include it in the login history record.
Add client_id to the LoginHistory entity for better audit tracking.
Enrich login history remarks with AJAX status and redirect URL for debugging purposes.
Move isAjax calculation earlier in the method to allow its value to be used in the login history remarks.

src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.general_idea: Refactor HttpSecurity configuration from the deprecated fluent API (using .and()) to the new lambda-based DSL introduced in Spring Security 6.
Explicitly define the security matcher for the filter chain to scope the security rules to specific URL patterns.
Add @Order annotation to the SecurityFilterChain bean to explicitly control the order of filter chain evaluation.
Configure OAuth2 Login support within the security filter chain, including login page and success handler.
Remove the autowired AuthenticationDetailsSource bean as it is no longer used in the new configuration style.
Replace the custom AuthenticationProvider bean with a private method returning a custom AuthenticationSuccessHandler for OAuth2 login.
Update imports to reflect the removal of unused classes and addition of new configuration classes.
src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.human_impl: Removed HttpServletRequest, AuthenticationDetailsSource, etc.; Added Order, AbstractHttpConfigurer, AuthenticationSuccessHandler
